_comment: GRC-parity block target manifest. Machine-readable source of truth for the status dashboard and the agent work-queue. Edit this (status, grc_block, params, notes); metrics are filled from verification reports by tools/gen_dashboard.py. Do NOT hand-edit STATUS.md. status: done | in_progress | planned | wont_map. tier: 1=feed-forward (autonomous), 2=stateful/loop, 3=new GRC block to build. Priority is tier-ascending then listed order.
_naming_principle: Names must match GNU Radio so users never guess the equivalent. (1) Each Kyttar block's GRC palette LABEL is its GNU Radio counterpart's label verbatim (e.g. 'FIR Filter'), keeping a 'Kyttar'/'kyttar_' marker ONLY where GRC namespacing requires it to coexist with stock GR. (2) 'grc_block' below names the EXACT GNU Radio factory (e.g. filter.fir_filter_fff) so the equivalence is explicit and searchable in the dashboard. A Kyttar block with no clean GNU Radio counterpart sets grc_block to '' and explains in notes.

blocks.notes: Reference example; fully documented. Single MULQ.

Zero-stuffing rate expander (front half of an interpolating pulse-shaper). Verified 1:1 vs interp_fir_filter_fff(sps,[1.0]): one input -> sample + sps-1 zeros, kept sample pass-through, zeros exact -> bit-exact in Q15. RATE-EXPANDING: verified with run_block_dut_rate (drains the full per-trigger burst; plain run_block_dut keeps only the last word). 9 tests (sps in {2,4,8}, full-scale edges, repeat-instead-of-zero + wrong-rate mutations). Single cell, unrolled WRITE/JUMP burst then HALT (JUMP does NOT halt the issuer).

PARITY-GAP: exposes hardware-internal freq_word instead of GR frequency+sample_rate; missing amplitude/phase. Siblings NCO/ComplexMixer already derive freq_word from Hz -> no excuse. Must expose real-world params.

Matches firdes.root_raised_cosine(gain,sampling_freq,symbol_rate,alpha,ntaps) VERBATIM via a bit-exact firdes port in _firdes.root_raised_cosine (max dev ~1e-8 across gain/fs/alpha/ntaps). Taps run on the verified FIR datapath; sps derived from sampling_freq/symbol_rate (was hardcoded 4). 15 tests: tap-parity, BPSK stream, impulse, alpha+ntaps+gain sweeps, inverted+wrong-alpha mutations. Old hand-rolled alpha/span formula removed.

Verified vs GNU Radio blocks.multiply_ff (19 tests). The generic two-stream real product out[n]=a[n]*b[n] (AM detect, squaring, error nodes) -- distinct from GainBlock (multiply by a CONSTANT) and ComplexMixerBlock (multiply by an internally-generated complex exponential). Single cell, single MULQ: out=(a*b)>>15. multiply_ff takes NO params so the block has none (full GRC parity); the 'param sweep' is over input amplitude regimes. The two streams reuse the proven complex-burst fan-in (Costas xi/xq tap): each sample = WRITE a->R0, WRITE b->R1, JUMP; the verify harness carries them as one complex array (real=a, imag=b) via run_block_dut_complex(in_ports=('a','b'), words_per_sample=1), DUT product lands in the I channel. Two-tier: DUT vs GR multiply_ff (AMPLITUDE, single-MULQ floor ~1 LSB) AND DUT vs process_reference_q15 (EXACT, the wrapping Q15 MULQ). Q15 OVERFLOW: the lone overflow is the exact (-1.0)*(-1.0)=+1.0 corner where MULQ WRAPS to -1.0 (the datapath wraps, does not saturate) -- a dedicated test drives that corner and asserts DUT==wrapping reference; the GR-equivalence stimulus stays off the simultaneous full-scale-negative corner. Multiply is COMMUTATIVE so a swapped-stream mutation is not a corruption (not tested, documented); mutations inverted/wrong-second-stream/halved-magnitude/+1-delay/empty all FAIL the gate (INV-4). GOTCHA: a {write:NAME}/{jump:NAME} placeholder must be ALONE on its line (resolver _RE_WRITE matches ^\s*\{write:(\w+)\}\s*$); a trailing inline comment leaves it unsubstituted and the assembler errors 'Unknown opcode {WRITE:OUT}'.

Verified vs GNU Radio blocks.add_ff (out=a+b of two real streams; 39 tests shared with SubtractBlock). Single cell: one ADD + a SATURATING clamp. add_ff has NO params (full GRC parity); the 'param sweep' is over in-range amplitude regimes. Two streams via the proven complex-burst fan-in (WRITE a->R0, WRITE b->R1, JUMP); verify harness run_block_dut_complex(in_ports=('a','b'), words_per_sample=1) carries them as one complex array (real=a, imag=b), product/sum in the I channel. PRODUCTION SATURATION (not wrap): the Q15 ALU ADD WRAPS on overflow (0.6+0.6 -> -0.8 sign flip), a footgun; this block instead SATURATES like the FIR/decimator/dc-blocker. Mechanism: ADD sets the V (signed-overflow) flag; on overflow the true result's sign == sign(a) (same-sign operands for add), so the rail is picked from a's sign bit via the shared 0x7FFF+signbit trick (+0x7FFF / -0x8000). Two-path structure (duplicated emit + terminal HALT, BR.V target on a REAL instruction) reuses the FIR's saturating-restore shape -- a branch onto a {write}/{jump} placeholder label is miscompiled to a stray output JUMP. Two-tier: DUT vs GR add_ff (AMPLITUDE, in-range |a+b|<1 where saturate==true sum, ~1 LSB) AND DUT vs process_reference_q15 (EXACT, the saturating add, incl. overflow corners). Saturation verified directly (out-of-range pins to +-full, no wrap). Add is COMMUTATIVE so a swapped-stream mutation is not a corruption (documented); mutations inverted/wrong-second-stream/+1-delay/empty all FAIL (INV-4). Shares add_block.py with SubtractBlock.

Verified vs GNU Radio blocks.sub_ff (out=a-b of two real streams). Same single-cell SATURATING combiner as AddBlock (shares add_block.py / _TwoStreamAddSub), with SUB instead of ADD. For subtract, overflow occurs on OPPOSITE-sign operands and the true result's sign is STILL sign(a) (a>0,b<0 -> +; a<0,b>0 -> -), so the identical sign(a)-rail saturation applies. Two-tier verification identical to AddBlock. Subtract is NOT commutative (a-b != b-a) so a swapped-stream mutation IS a corruption and is tested (must FAIL), in addition to inverted/wrong-second-stream/+1-delay/empty. add_cc/sub_cc (complex, 4 external operands) deferred to Tier-2 -- the proven 2-operand fan-in cannot deliver four (see TIER1_BLOCK_BACKLOG deferred section).

Verified vs GNU Radio blocks.complex_to_float (re=real(in), im=imag(in); 20 tests shared with FloatToComplexBlock). On the Kyttar substrate a complex sample IS a two-operand (re@R0, im@R1) pair, so this conversion is the IDENTITY datapath: read the pair, emit it as two words (re then im) on one bus corridor, de-interleaved by the harness into I/Q (the NCO/mixer two-word-egress pattern). Pure data movement (MOVE/WRITE, NO arithmetic) so it is EXACT -- zero Q15 error -- and the gate is bit-exact (metric=exact) on BOTH channels. Single cell, words_per_sample=2. Mutations (swapped re/im, negated imag/conjugate, +1 delay, empty) all FAIL (INV-4). Shares _IQPassthrough/complex_float_block.py with FloatToComplexBlock.

Verified vs GNU Radio blocks.float_to_complex (out=re+j*im of two real streams). The complement of ComplexToFloatBlock and the SAME identity datapath (shares _IQPassthrough/complex_float_block.py): the two real inputs (re@R0, im@R1) are emitted as the two words of one complex sample. EXACT, bit-exact gate on both channels, single cell, words_per_sample=2. The complex<->float pair is the most common type conversion in any I/Q graph. Mutations (swapped re/im, negated imag, +1 delay, empty) all FAIL.

Verified vs GNU Radio blocks.complex_to_mag_squared (out=re^2+im^2; 21 tests). The instantaneous-power / envelope primitive (energy detectors, AGC error, squelch). Single cell, two Q15 ops: MULQ re,re then MACQ im,im accumulate (re^2+im^2) in R0. No params (full GRC parity). SATURATION: the power range [0,2) overflows Q15's [0,1) for |z|>=1 (the unit circle), so the block SATURATES to +full-scale. The power is ALWAYS >=0, so any overflow makes the 16-bit accumulator look negative (bit15 set) -- detected with a single BR.N and pinned to 0x7FFF (a cheaper one-sided clamp than the add/sub sign-rail; no satpos sign math needed). Two-tier: DUT vs GR (AMPLITUDE, IN-RANGE |z|<1 where the result is representable, op_count=2 floor=3 LSB, measured ~2 LSB from MULQ/MACQ truncation vs GR's rounded square) AND DUT vs process_reference_q15 ((re^2>>15)+(im^2>>15) saturating, EXACT, incl. overflow corners). Saturation verified directly (|z|>=1 pins to +full, no wrap). |z|^2 is SYMMETRIC in re/im so a swapped-channel mutation is not a corruption (documented); mutations inverted/halved/wrong-second-stream/+1-delay/empty all FAIL (INV-4). complex_to_mag (sqrt) and complex_to_arg (atan2) of backlog #4 are DEFERRED to Tier-2 (need new sqrt/CORDIC machinery -- see TIER1_BLOCK_BACKLOG).

Verified vs GNU Radio blocks.conjugate_cc (out=conj(in)=re-j*im; 11 tests). The correlator / conjugate-multiply staple. Single cell: pass re through, negate im (0-im via SUB), emit the two words; words_per_sample=2. No params (full GRC parity). EXACT gate (0 LSB vs GR on both channels) -- pure data movement + one negate. Q15 corner: the only imag whose negate overflows is exactly -1.0 (0x8000): -(-1.0)=+1.0 is unrepresentable so SUB WRAPS to 0x8000 (the bit-exact reference models the wrap; a dedicated test pins it). GR-equivalence stimulus stays off im=-1.0 so the conjugate is exact. Key mutation (teeth): a NOT-conjugated DUT (im passed through un-negated) FAILS -- proves the block actually conjugates, not echoes; plus swapped-channels/+1-delay/empty all FAIL (INV-4).

Verified vs GNU Radio blocks.abs_ff (out=|in|; 9 tests). Single cell, one conditional negate (CMP vs 0, then 0-in if negative -- the AGC/QAM16-slicer abs idiom; BR.NN target on a REAL instruction). Single real input via run_block_dut, single real output. No params (full GRC parity). Result is EXACT (0 LSB vs GR) except the lone in=-1.0 (0x8000) corner: |-1.0|=+1.0 unrepresentable so the negate WRAPS to 0x8000=-1.0 -- the bit-exact reference models the wrap (a dedicated test pins it) and the GR-equivalence stimulus stays off it. Key mutation (teeth): a NON-rectified DUT (a negative passed through) FAILS, proving the block takes the magnitude; plus +1-delay/empty FAIL (INV-4). NOTE the backlog #7 'negate' (unary sign flip) is GainBlock(gain=-1) -- already covered, not a separate block; analog.rms_cf is deferred to Tier-2 (needs the deferred Q15 sqrt + a stateful averager).

Verified vs GNU Radio blocks.keep_one_in_n (keep 1 sample of every n, no filter; 26 tests). A pure pass-through gated by a modulo-n counter -- the DecimatorBlock's emit gate WITHOUT the FIR. Single cell. Param mirrors GR: n (keep factor). PHASE: GR keeps the LAST sample of each group of n (verified empirically: keep_one_in_n(3) of 0..11 -> 2,5,8,11 = phase n-1), so the up-counter emits when it reaches n then resets (XOR self), matching GR. EXACT pass-through (no Q15 arithmetic) -> bit-exact gate; emitted samples equal the input bit-for-bit. run_block_dut records None on dropped triggers so the kept stream is outputs[n-1::n]; the emit-phase contract (emit iff i%n==n-1) is asserted directly across n in {1..5}. Mutations (wrong n, wrong phase n-2, +1 delay, empty) all FAIL (INV-4). Counter is a persistent StateVar (init 0); the skip path is a bare HALT label (a REAL instruction, not a {write}/{jump} placeholder -- build-engine GOTO miscompile). repeat (the upsampling twin) and interleave/deinterleave deferred to Tier-2 (see TIER1_BLOCK_BACKLOG).

Verified vs GNU Radio blocks.moving_average_ff (out=scale*sum of last `length` samples; 18 tests). A moving average IS an FIR whose `length` taps are all equal to `scale`, so MovingAverageBlock SUBCLASSES the verified FIRFilterBlock with constant box taps (the LowPassFilter pattern) -- all Q15 datapath / multi-cell fold / COEFFICIENT-HEADROOM saturation inherited unchanged. Constant taps are symmetric so the reversed-tap convention is moot and group delay=0, aligned with GR's causal running sum. Params mirror GRC's Moving Average: length + scale (GR's max_iter is an output-buffer bound and vlen=1 -- neither affects the sample math, so neither is a Kyttar param). scale=1/length is a true average (Sum|tap|=1 -> S=0); a larger scale engages the inherited saturating headroom restore (S>0). Two-tier: DUT vs GR moving_average_ff (AMPLITUDE, headroom-aware q15_quant_floor(length,S)) AND DUT vs the inherited process_reference_q15 (EXACT). Coverage: edge + 3 random + length sweep {2,3,5,8,16} (true average) + bit-exact incl. S=1 headroom (3,0.5)/(6,0.3); mutations inverted/wrong-length/+1-delay/empty all FAIL (INV-4). KNOWN LIMIT (inherited FIR per-cell budget): a SINGLE-cell box with S>0 caps at ~3 taps -- a 4-tap box at scale 0.5 (4 taps + S=1 restore on one cell) raises at build; use scale<=1/length (S=0) or a longer/multi-cell length. Not a moving-average-specific limit.

Verified vs GNU Radio blocks.complex_to_real (out=real(in); 18 tests shared with ComplexToImagBlock). A complex sample is carried as a two-operand (re@R0, im@R1) pair; this selector forwards the RE operand as its single real output (words_per_sample=1). A subset of complex_to_float but a separate GR block people wire directly to grab one rail. Pure data movement (one MOVE) -> EXACT (0 LSB vs GR). No params. Single cell, shares _ComplexSelect/complex_real_imag_block.py with ComplexToImagBlock. Key mutation (teeth): selecting the WRONG rail (real DUT vs GR imag reference) FAILS, proving it picks the correct channel; plus +1-delay/empty FAIL (INV-4).

Verified vs GNU Radio blocks.complex_to_imag (out=imag(in)). The IM-channel twin of ComplexToRealBlock and the SAME selector datapath (shares _ComplexSelect/complex_real_imag_block.py): forwards the im operand (R1) as the single real output. EXACT (0 LSB), single cell, words_per_sample=1. Mutations (wrong rail = imag DUT vs GR real, +1-delay, empty) all FAIL.

Verified vs filter.dc_blocker_ff. GR's DC blocker is LTI -- a SYMMETRIC FIR -- so DCBlockerBlock SUBCLASSES the verified FIRFilterBlock with the dc-blocker impulse response as its coefficients. Reverse-engineered from GR impulse/step response and confirmed bit-for-bit (float, <1e-4) for D in {2,4,8,16,32}, both forms: SHORT form (long_form=False) = x[n-(D-1)] - MA_D^2(x) (two cascaded length-D moving averagers; triangular kernel, 2D-1 taps, group delay D-1); LONG form (long_form=True, GR default) = x[n-(2D-2)] - MA_D^4(x) (four cascaded; 4D-3 taps, group delay 2D-2). Sum(taps)=0 (true DC notch); taps symmetric so the FIR reversed-tap convention is moot. Params mirror GR's GRC dc_blocker_xx VERBATIM: length (GR's D, default 32) + long_form (default True). GR default = 125 taps = 26 cells; folds 7x4 (<=8 across, INV-9), routes + sims + matches GR end-to-end. TOLERANCE: the dc-blocker taps have Sum|h|~1.5..2, so COEFFICIENT HEADROOM (INV-13) engages with S=1 (coeffs pre-scaled by 1/2, saturating-shift gain restore -> the block SATURATES on overload, no rollover). S=1 costs ~1 bit of coeff precision, so the DUT-vs-GR amplitude floor is the HEADROOM-AWARE q15_quant_floor(N, head_shift=S) = N*(2^(S-1)+1)+1 LSB (=2N+1 at S=1), NOT the plain N+1 -- a DERIVED fixed-point worst case (empirically bounds the error with ~18% margin), not a loosened gate. Two-tier verification like the FIR: DUT vs GR float (amplitude, headroom floor) AND DUT vs process_reference_q15 (EXACT, models the saturating datapath). Coverage: taps-vs-GR-impulse, edge + 3 random + (length,long_form) sweep single->26 cells, bit-exact multicell, overload-saturation (sign-aligned full-scale pins the rails) + wrap-mutation, and inverted/wrong-length/+1-delay/empty/deep-cell mutations all FAIL the gate. Fixed a latent FIR _fold_geometry bug (n=26 had only the H=1 even-column fold -> a 26x1 off-grid LINE; capped even-column folds to <=8 across, INV-9) and extended INV-11 params-threading to the GUI port-stub/flyline renderer (chip_canvas resolved port geometry param-less, dropping a scaling block's last-cell output stub). All placekyt callers updated to a 1-cell instance (length=2, long_form=False) so geometry-sensitive fixtures are unchanged; full suite 937 passed.

Now owns GR's decim AND interp params (fir_filter_fff(decim,taps) / interp_fir_filter_fff(interp,taps)) -- decimation folded in (the standalone DecimatorBlock was removed; it was an INVENTED block, decimation is a GR PARAMETER). decim: mod-M output gate, verified bit-exact on-chip + vs GR (test_decimator.py). interp: zero-stuff burst, verified vs interp_fir_filter (1 LSB). HW-DEVIATION (decim>1 only): mod-M counter shares the output cell with the saturating restore -> Sum|h|<=4 (raises above); a plain/interp FIR has no such limit. HW-DEVIATION (interp>1): single-cell unrolled-burst tap cap (L=2->4 taps etc.); larger interp FIRs compose Upsampler->FIR (raises with that message). Plain-FIR arbitrary-taps path verified vs GR.

PARITY-GAP: fixed single biquad (3 fftaps + 2 fbtaps); GR filter.iir_filter_ffd takes ARBITRARY-length tap lists (any order) + oldstyle flag. Higher order achievable via cascaded cells -> not a hard ISA wall. Must generalize or document a justified HW limit.
PARITY-GAP: missing oscillator amplitude (NCO exposes it - inconsistent), phase, offset vs analog.sig_source_c. Add them.

Matches analog.sig_source_c(samp_rate,COS,freq,amp,offset,phase) — added offset (real DC bias on I only, matching GR real-offset behaviour) + phase (initial phase radians -> 16-bit accumulator start). Real-world Hz params (NOT a freq_word proxy). 33-entry quarter-wave LUT + interp -> ~1-2 LSB on grid; ~11 LSB derived table floor off-grid. 26 tests incl. offset/phase sweep vs GR.

PARITY-GAP: hardwired BPSK (LLR=coeff*I); GR digital.constellation_soft_decoder_cf is CONSTELLATION-DRIVEN (takes a constellation object, complex input, per-bit LLRs). No constellation param exposed. Must generalize.
Proof-of-concept: works in the coherent BPSK RX demo but has NOT been through per-block GRC-equivalence verification. Param parity: samp_rate/sym_rate/alpha/ntaps.

GRC-verbatim params (rate, reference, gain, max_gain); update law gain += rate*(reference-|out|). Verified vs analog.agc_ff within ~39 LSB in the ATTENUATING regime (gain<=1, Q15 range). Amplification (gain>1) needs a gain register with integer headroom — out of scope for the single-cell Q15 block.
Proof-of-concept: works in the coherent BPSK RX demo but NOT yet per-block verified. Carrier recovery loop. Lock-detector + tracking-error metric, not sample-by-sample. Param: add order.
Proof-of-concept: works in the coherent BPSK RX demo but NOT yet per-block verified. Timing recovery loop. Worst-offender param refactor (kp/ki -> sps/loop_bw).
Proof-of-concept: works in the coherent BPSK RX demo but NOT yet per-block verified. Decision output; BER metric, not NMSE on packed words.

PARITY-GAP: ramp param raises (UNIMPLEMENTED, not a HW limit) - GR analog.pwr_squelch_ff supports a sinusoidal ramp; must implement. gate=True is a genuine HW limit (one-output-per-input) - keep raising but must be documented LOUDLY per INV-0.

PARITY-GAP: hardcoded 3-mode enum (bpsk/qpsk/8psk); GR digital.chunks_to_symbols_bc takes an ARBITRARY symbol_table + dimensionality. A 32-entry constellation table fits the ISA (LOAD supports 32 entries) -> the 3-mode limit is ARBITRARY, not hardware. Must generalize. Only BPSK was ever verified.
Deterministic given seed; exact compare, NO correlation (no meaningful peak).
NEW: complex gain. Trivial extension of GainBlock to I/Q.
NEW: single ADD with an immediate.
NEW: FM demod (atan2 of conjugate product). Common, high value.
NEW: mixer + decimating FIR fused. Workhorse channelizer block.

Verified vs GNU Radio firdes.low_pass + fir_filter_fff (31 tests; full verification suite 155; placekyt 937/16-skip). A convenience FIR specified in DSP units, so LowPassFilter SUBCLASSES the verified FIRFilterBlock (all Q15 coefficient-headroom/saturation/multi-cell-fold machinery inherited) with firdes-designed taps. Params mirror GRC's Low Pass Filter VERBATIM: gain, samp_rate, cutoff_freq(Hz), transition_width(Hz), window, beta(Kaiser). KEY CONSTRAINT: GR is NOT importable in the runtime .venv (ships only on the verification host), so the block CANNOT call gnuradio.filter.firdes -- it REIMPLEMENTS firdes in pure Python (runtime/.../blocks/_firdes.py): windowed-sinc + the same compute_ntaps (atten*fs/(22*tw) -> odd), the gr::fft::window builders (Hamming/Hann/Blackman/Rectangular/Blackman-Harris cos-windows + Kaiser via Izero Bessel series), and the unity-gain normalization, all op-for-op (double product -> float32 per tap, double fmax accumulation, float*=double restore). FAITHFULNESS: tap count exact; FLOAT taps reproduce firdes to ~1 float32 ULP -- NOT bit-exact across the run boundary because the customer-modem .venv links a DIFFERENT libm than the GR host so sin/cos differ in the last bit (and GR's C++ coswindow uses FMA), a sub-ULP difference far below a Q15 LSB; the Q15-QUANTIZED taps (what reaches the chip) are BIT-EXACT to firdes for EVERY window (verified across hamming/hann/blackman/rectangular/kaiser/blackman_harris x 3 configs), so the on-chip filter IS provably the firdes filter. firdes low-pass taps are linear-phase SYMMETRIC (reversed-tap convention moot; delay=0). Tolerance = inherited headroom-aware q15_quant_floor(N, head_shift=S); a normalized low-pass has Sum|h| slightly >1 (sidelobes) so S=1 is typical (default 39-tap fs32k/co4k/tw2k = 9 cells, S=1). Two-tier: DUT vs GR firdes->fir_filter_fff end-to-end (AMPLITUDE, headroom floor) AND DUT vs FIRFilterBlock.process_reference_q15 (EXACT). Mutations (inverted, wrong-cutoff, +1 delay, empty) all FAIL the gate. GRC: kyttar_low_pass_filter.block.yml label 'Low Pass Filter' (+ kyttar.low_pass_filter marker wrapper); grc_import maps kyttar_low_pass_filter -> LowPassFilter via the snake->Pascal fallback (no override). _firdes.py is shared by the High/Band-pass + Band-reject convenience filters.

Verified vs GNU Radio firdes.high_pass + fir_filter_fff (30 tests; full verification suite 185; placekyt 937/16-skip). Same pattern as LowPassFilter: SUBCLASSES FIRFilterBlock with firdes-designed taps from the shared pure-Python blocks/_firdes.py (GR absent in the runtime .venv). firdes high_pass is normalized to unity gain at NYQUIST (fmax accumulates taps[n]*cos(n*pi)); taps linear-phase SYMMETRIC (delay=0, reversed-tap convention moot). Params mirror GRC's High Pass Filter VERBATIM: gain, samp_rate, cutoff_freq(Hz), transition_width(Hz), window, beta. Q15-quantized taps BIT-EXACT to firdes for ALL six windows (hamming/hann/blackman/rectangular/kaiser/blackman_harris x 3 configs) -> on-chip filter IS the firdes filter (INV-16); float taps ~1 ULP (cross-interpreter libm). A firdes high-pass has Sum|h|>1 (large centre tap + negative sidelobes) so COEFFICIENT HEADROOM engages (default 39-tap fs32k/co4k/tw2k = 9 cells, S=1). Two-tier: DUT vs GR firdes->fir_filter_fff (AMPLITUDE, headroom floor) AND DUT vs process_reference_q15 (EXACT). Mutations (inverted, wrong-cutoff, +1 delay, empty) all FAIL. GRC: kyttar_high_pass_filter.block.yml label 'High Pass Filter' + kyttar.high_pass_filter wrapper.

Verified vs GNU Radio firdes.band_pass + fir_filter_fff (30 tests; full verification suite 215; placekyt 937/16-skip). Same pattern as LowPassFilter/HighPassFilter: SUBCLASSES FIRFilterBlock with firdes-designed taps from the shared pure-Python blocks/_firdes.py (GR absent in the runtime .venv). Takes TWO cutoffs: low_cutoff_freq + high_cutoff_freq (Hz). firdes band_pass is normalized to unity gain at the band CENTRE (fmax accumulates taps[n]*cos(n*freq), freq=pi*(lo+hi)/fs); taps linear-phase SYMMETRIC (delay=0). Params mirror GRC's Band Pass Filter VERBATIM. Q15-quantized taps BIT-EXACT to firdes for ALL six windows (x3 configs) -> on-chip filter IS the firdes filter (INV-16); float ~1 ULP. Sum|h|>1 so COEFFICIENT HEADROOM engages (default 39-tap fs32k/4k-8k/tw2k = 9 cells, S=1). Two-tier: DUT vs GR firdes->fir_filter_fff (AMPLITUDE, headroom floor) AND DUT vs process_reference_q15 (EXACT). Mutations (inverted, wrong-band, +1 delay, empty) all FAIL. GRC: kyttar_band_pass_filter.block.yml label 'Band Pass Filter' + kyttar.band_pass_filter wrapper.

Verified vs GNU Radio firdes.band_reject + fir_filter_fff (30 tests; full verification suite 245; placekyt 937/16-skip). Band-stop/notch; same pattern as the other firdes filters: SUBCLASSES FIRFilterBlock with taps from the shared blocks/_firdes.py (GR absent in the runtime .venv). Takes low_cutoff_freq + high_cutoff_freq (Hz); normalized to unity gain at DC (fmax accumulates taps[n], like low_pass). Taps linear-phase SYMMETRIC (delay=0). Params mirror GRC's Band Reject Filter VERBATIM. Q15-quantized taps BIT-EXACT to firdes for ALL six windows (x3 configs) -> on-chip filter IS the firdes filter (INV-16); float ~1 ULP. The notch has a LARGE centre tap so Sum|h|>2 -> COEFFICIENT HEADROOM S=2 (the highest of the four firdes filters; default 39-tap fs32k/4k-8k/tw2k = 9 cells, the FIR's last cell caps its segment to fit the S=2 saturating-shift restore, EXERCISING the FIR's S>=2 budget path end-to-end). Two-tier: DUT vs GR firdes->fir_filter_fff (AMPLITUDE, headroom floor) AND DUT vs process_reference_q15 (EXACT). Mutations (inverted, wrong-band, +1 delay, empty) all FAIL. GRC: kyttar_band_reject_filter.block.yml label 'Band Reject Filter' + kyttar.band_reject_filter wrapper. COMPLETES the four firdes convenience filters (Decision B).
